mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate dropping below 6.0% signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses below 6.0%, or the t-statistic for the return difference is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high retail sales growth and accelerating inflation indicate demand-pull pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both exceed their 6-month moving averages, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects supply chain bottlenecks rather than demand weakness, causing short-term equity volatility and negative 5-day returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during easing cycles yield positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with elevated housing starts signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of high-GDP-growth periods, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment surge exceeding 300,000 signals rapid labor market normalization that boosts near-term consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 300k in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the average return difference is statistically indistinguishable from zero with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside near-zero policy rates creates a negative real yield environment that compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of observations, or the interaction term loses predictive power when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment indicates productive capacity expansion that improves profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict positive returns when INDPRO growth is driven solely by inventory restocking, with out-of-sample hit rate below 48%.
